question: Is the header "INGREDIENTS:" correctly spelled and visible in the top section?
Is the text "Whole Wheat Flour (≥55%)" correctly spelled, including the "≥" symbol and parentheses?
Are the ingredients "Refined Vegetable Oil, Glucose Syrup, Maltodextrin, Whole Milk Powder, Fresh Eggs, Salt" correctly spelled?
Are the chemical ingredients "Sodium Bicarbonate, Ammonium Bicarbonate, Soy Lecithin, Natural Flavoring" correctly spelled?
Is the header "ALLERGEN INFORMATION:" correctly spelled and visible?
Is the allergen text "Contains wheat and other gluten-containing cereals, eggs, milk and dairy products, soy and soy derivatives" correctly spelled?
Is the allergen text "Manufactured in a facility that also processes tree nuts and peanuts" correctly spelled?
Is the warning "Not recommended for individuals with nut allergies" correctly spelled?
Is the header "NUTRITIONAL CLAIMS:" correctly spelled and visible in the middle section?
Is the sentence "This product is high in dietary fiber, which contributes to normal bowel function" correctly spelled?
Is the sentence "Contains 8.5g of dietary fiber per 100g, providing 34% of daily recommended intake" correctly spelled (including numbers)?
Is the sentence "Low-sugar formula designed for those monitoring blood glucose levels" correctly spelled?
Is the sentence "Diabetics should consult a physician before consumption and adjust intake accordingly" correctly spelled?
Is the header "STORAGE CONDITIONS & SHELF LIFE:" correctly spelled and visible in the bottom section?
Is the instruction "Store in a cool, dry place away from direct sunlight, heat, and humidity" correctly spelled?
Is the instruction "Once opened, consume promptly to prevent moisture absorption and loss of freshness" correctly spelled?
Is the text "Shelf life: 12 months (unopened)" correctly spelled?
Is the instruction "See production date and batch number printed on package seal" correctly spelled?
Is the warning "Do not purchase or consume if packaging is damaged or bloated" correctly spelled?
Are the text blocks arranged in the correct vertical order: Ingredients (top), Nutritional Claims (middle), and Storage (bottom)?
Is the "NUTRITIONAL CLAIMS" text positioned beside a nutrition facts panel?
Are the text characters free from garbled strokes, missing parts, or unrecognizable symbols?
Does the text follow the natural curvature and perspective of the packaging surface?
Is the text sharp and legible, avoiding excessive blur or low resolution?
Is the image free from unnatural visual artifacts (smudging, ghosting, random noise) around the text?
